---
title: "hierarchial_model"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggplot2))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(bayesplot))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(cmdstanr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(f1dataR))
```

```{r load-data-fallback}

## The dataset full_data.csv is included in the repository. 
##If you need to regenerate it from scratch using the f1dataR package, 
##use the following code (note this takes a long time to run):

# full_data <- map_dfr(2019:2024, function(year) {
#   schedule <- load_schedule(season = year)
#   map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(schedule)), function(round) {
#     tryCatch(
#       load_results(season = year, round = round) %>%
#         mutate(
#           season = year,
#           round = round,
#           race_name = schedule$race_name[round],
#           circuit = schedule$circuit_id[round],
#           top_speed_kph = as.numeric(top_speed_kph),
#           fastest_rank = as.integer(fastest_rank)
#         ),
#       error = function(e) {
#         message("Skipping season ", year, " round ", round, ": ", e$message)
#         NULL
#       }
#     )
#   })
# })
# 
# write_csv(full_data, "full_data.csv")

```


```{r load-data}
## Loading and preprocessing data

data <- read.csv("full_data.csv")

convert_to_seconds <- function(x) {
  if (is.numeric(x)) return(x)
  x <- as.character(x)
  sapply(x, function(val) {
    if (grepl("^[+-]?\\d+\\.?\\d*$", val)) {
      return(as.numeric(val))
    }
    if (!grepl("^[+-]", val) && grepl(":", val)) {
      return(0)
    }
    sign <- ifelse(startsWith(val, "-"), -1, 1)
    val <- gsub("^[+-]", "", val)
    parts <- strsplit(val, ":")[[1]]
    if (length(parts) == 2) {
      return(sign * (as.numeric(parts[1]) * 60 + as.numeric(parts[2])))
    } else if (length(parts) == 3) {
      return(sign * (as.numeric(parts[1]) * 3600 + as.numeric(parts[2]) * 60 + as.numeric(parts[3])))
    } else {
      return(NA)
    }
  }, USE.NAMES = FALSE)
}

data <- data %>%
  mutate(gap = convert_to_seconds(gap),
         grid = as.integer(grid)) %>%
  filter(
    status == "Finished",
    !is.na(grid),
    grid > 0,
    !is.na(gap))

# head(data)
```

```{r circuit-index}
## Indexing circuit for stan

circuit_levels <- sort(unique(data$circuit))

data <- data |>
  mutate(circuit_id = as.integer(factor(circuit, levels = circuit_levels)))

N_circuits <- length(circuit_levels)
N_obs      <- nrow(data)

cat("Total observations:", N_obs, "\n")
cat("Number of circuits:", N_circuits, "\n")
cat("Circuit index range:", min(data$circuit_id), "to", max(data$circuit_id), "\n")

head(data)

# data |> distinct(circuit, circuit_id) |> arrange(circuit_id)
```

```{r eda-sample-size}
# Sample size per circuit

data |>
  count(circuit) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(circuit, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#a93226", color = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Sample size per circuit",
    x     = "Circuit",
    y     = "Number of observations"
  )
```

```{r eda-heatmap}
data |>
  mutate(grid_group = cut(grid, breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
                          labels = c("1-5", "6-10", "11-15", "16-20"))) |>
  group_by(circuit, grid_group) |>
  summarise(median_gap = median(gap), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = grid_group, y = reorder(circuit, median_gap), fill = median_gap)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#00d3ff", "#ffffff", "#e10600"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(0, 30, 120)),  # adjust midpoint if needed
    labels = scales::label_number(suffix = "s"),
    name = "Avg Gap"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Median gap by circuit and grid position group",
    x     = "Grid position group",
    y     = "Circuit",
    fill  = "Median gap (s)"
  )
```

```{r fit-mcmc, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='hide'}
# hierarchical Stan model and fit using MCMC with 5 chains

dir.create(file.path("stan_out_hierarchical"), showWarnings = FALSE)

grid_pred_pos    <- 10
circuit_pred_pos <- 1

mod_hierarchical <- cmdstan_model("hierarchical_model_exp.stan")

fit_hierarchical <- mod_hierarchical$sample(
  seed          = 1,
  chains        = 5,
  refresh       = 0,
  output_dir    = "stan_out_hierarchical",
  data          = list(
    N            = N_obs,
    J            = N_circuits,
    circuit_id   = data$circuit_id,
    grid         = data$grid,
    gap          = data$gap,
    grid_pred    = grid_pred_pos,
    circuit_pred = circuit_pred_pos
  ),
  iter_warmup   = 500,
  iter_sampling = 500
)

# print(fit_hierarchical)
```

```{r fit-vi, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, results='hide'}
# hierarchical model using variational inference

fit_hierarchical_vi <- mod_hierarchical$variational(
  seed             = 1,
  refresh          = 0,
  show_messages    = FALSE,
  show_exceptions  = FALSE,
  output_dir       = "stan_out_hierarchical",
  algorithm        = "fullrank",
  output_samples   = 1000,
  data             = list(
    N            = N_obs,
    J            = N_circuits,
    circuit_id   = data$circuit_id,
    grid         = data$grid,
    gap          = data$gap,
    grid_pred    = grid_pred_pos,
    circuit_pred = circuit_pred_pos
  )
)
```

```{r model-summary, warning=FALSE}
# ========= model summary (ESS and R hat) ================
print(fit_hierarchical)
fit_hierarchical$summary(NULL, c("mean", "sd", "mcse_mean", "ess_bulk"))

print(fit_hierarchical_vi)
fit_hierarchical_vi$summary(NULL, c("mean", "sd", "mcse_mean", "ess_bulk"))

```

```{r calibration-setup}
# Setup calibration parameters and sample validation observations
set.seed(1)
N_train = N_obs - 1

ci_level = 0.8
ci_plims = c((1 - ci_level) / 2, (1 + ci_level) / 2)

validation_obs <- sample(1:N_obs, 50, replace = FALSE)
```

```{r calibration-mcmc-loop, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# MCMC leave-one-out calibration loop over 50 validation observations
all_mcmc_samples <- list()
ci_limits_hier = matrix(0, nrow = 50, 2)

for (i in 1:length(validation_obs)) {
  i_test = validation_obs[i]
  train_test_dta = list(
    N            = N_train,
    J            = N_circuits,
    circuit_id   = data$circuit_id[-i_test],
    grid         = data$grid[-i_test],
    gap          = data$gap[-i_test],
    grid_pred    = data$grid[i_test],
    circuit_pred = data$circuit_id[i_test]
  )
  mcmc_results = mod_hierarchical$sample(
    seed             = 1,
    data             = train_test_dta,
    output_dir       = "stan_out_hierarchical",
    refresh          = 0,
    show_messages    = FALSE,
    show_exceptions  = FALSE,
    chains           = 1,
    iter_warmup      = 500,
    iter_sampling    = 500
  )
  hier_samples           = as.vector(mcmc_results$draws("gap_pred"))
  all_mcmc_samples[[i]] = as.vector(mcmc_results$draws("gap_pred"))
  ci_limits_hier[i, ]   = quantile(hier_samples, ci_plims)
}
```

```{r calibration-mcmc-plot}
# MCMC calibration plot showing 80% credible intervals vs observed gaps
merged_data_hier = data[validation_obs, ] |>
  bind_cols(data.frame(CI_L = ci_limits_hier[, 1], CI_R = ci_limits_hier[, 2])) |>
  mutate(Inside_CI = (gap >= CI_L & gap <= CI_R))

merged_data_hier |>
  ggplot(aes(x = grid, y = gap, ymin = CI_L, ymax = CI_R, color = Inside_CI)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hierarchical MCMC posterior predictive calibration (80% CI)",
       x = "Grid Position", y = "Gap to Winner (seconds)")

mean(merged_data_hier$Inside_CI)
```

```{r calibration-vi-loop, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# VI leave-one-out calibration loop over 50 validation observations with clipping
all_vi_samples <- list()
ci_limits_hier_var = matrix(0, nrow = 50, 2)

for (i in 1:length(validation_obs)) {
  i_test = validation_obs[i]
  train_test_dta = list(
    N            = N_train,
    J            = N_circuits,
    circuit_id   = data$circuit_id[-i_test],
    grid         = data$grid[-i_test],
    gap          = data$gap[-i_test],
    grid_pred    = data$grid[i_test],
    circuit_pred = data$circuit_id[i_test]
  )
  vi_results = mod_hierarchical$variational(
    seed           = 1,
    data           = train_test_dta,
    output_dir     = "stan_out_hierarchical",
    refresh        = 0,
    show_messages  = FALSE,
    show_exceptions = FALSE,
    algorithm      = "fullrank",
    output_samples = 1000
  )
  hier_var_samples        = as.vector(vi_results$draws("gap_pred"))
  # hier_var_samples        = pmin(hier_var_samples, 500)
  all_vi_samples[[i]]     = hier_var_samples
  ci_limits_hier_var[i, ] = quantile(hier_var_samples, ci_plims)
}
```

```{r calibration-vi-plot}
# VI calibration plot showing 80% credible intervals vs observed gaps
merged_data_hier_var = data[validation_obs, ] |>
  bind_cols(data.frame(CI_L = ci_limits_hier_var[, 1], CI_R = ci_limits_hier_var[, 2])) |>
  mutate(Inside_CI = (gap >= CI_L & gap <= CI_R))

merged_data_hier_var |>
  ggplot(aes(x = grid, y = gap, ymin = CI_L, ymax = CI_R, color = Inside_CI)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Grid", y = "Gap")

mean(merged_data_hier_var$Inside_CI)
print("end")
```

```{r trace-mcmc}
# Trace plots for global and circuit-level parameters to assess MCMC mixing
dir.create("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

p1 <- mcmc_trace(fit_hierarchical$draws("alpha")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Trace plot: alpha (MCMC) — Hierarchical")
ggsave("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical/trace_mcmc_Hierarchical_alpha.png", p1, width = 8, height = 4)

p2 <- mcmc_trace(fit_hierarchical$draws("mu_beta")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Trace plot: mu_beta (MCMC) — Hierarchical")
ggsave("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical/trace_mcmc_Hierarchical_mu_beta.png", p2, width = 8, height = 4)

p3 <- mcmc_trace(fit_hierarchical$draws("sigma_beta")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Trace plot: sigma_beta (MCMC) — Hierarchical")
ggsave("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical/trace_mcmc_Hierarchical_sigma_beta.png", p3, width = 8, height = 4)

p4 <- mcmc_trace(fit_hierarchical$draws("beta_j[1]")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Trace plot: beta_j[1] (MCMC) — Hierarchical")
ggsave("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical/trace_mcmc_Hierarchical_beta_j1.png", p4, width = 8, height = 4)

p5 <- mcmc_trace(fit_hierarchical$draws("beta_j[6]")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Trace plot: beta_j[6] (MCMC) — Hierarchical")
  ggsave("plots/trace_plots_Hierarchical/trace_mcmc_Hierarchical_beta_j6.png", p5, width = 8, height = 4)

p1; p2; p3; p4; p5
```
